filename: Copy of Image processing benchmarking  Project.md
relative_path: Data & Figures/Data/1. t-CycIF/4_4 - PRITTI cohort experiment/4_4 analysis files/5. Arttus_internship/Copy of Image processing benchmarking  Project.md
full_text:
Processes to be targeted

- Denoising/ Background correction

- Signal enhancement filters

- Segmentation metrics

 - Intersection over union (threshold)

 - F1 score

 - Average precision

 - Precision recall curve

- Data normalization

- Compression formats

\###############################################################

Week 1: CREATING THE BENCHMARKING DATASET

- Get familiar with tCycIF image handling and markers to be utilized

- Crop relevant areas in tCycIF images - 4 (Tumor, Stroma, TLS,
 > macrophage-rich)

- Run Mesmer and Stardist on the crops to get some reference masks

- Outcome: A **dataset of annotated reference images** for
 > benchmarking.

Week 2-3: FILTER EXPLORATION AND PARAMETER TESTING

- Select Preprocessing Filters to Evaluate

 - Suggestions include:

 - Gaussian Blur, Unsharp Masking, CLAHE, Gamma correction:
 > Decide on parameters to be tested and optimized to improve
 > segmentation (over DAPI channel)

 - PENGUIN denoising: [[Welcome to PENGUIN's documentation! ---
 > PENGUIN 0.1
 > documentation]{.underline}](https://demirandalab-penguin.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)

- Create code to test on this approach and benchmark the times of
 > computation

- Outcome:

 - Computational costs (RAM, time)

 - Preprocessed images with different filtering methods

 - Initial observations on how filters/parameters affect image
 > features.

Outcome:

 - Computational costs (RAM, time)

 - Preprocessed images with different filtering methods

 - Initial observations on how filters/parameters affect image
 > features.

Week 4: SEGMENTATION METHODS

- Select segmentation methods to be applied (Stardist,Cellpose,Mesmer)

- Run on all filtered and unfiltered crops

- Outcome

 - Segmentation results on crops for **method combination**.

 - Image-based observations on how do these affect

 - segmentation quality

 - signal range

 - are there cell-type-specific changes?

Week 5-6: PERFORMANCE ASSESSMENT

- Select Benchmark Metrics

- Evaluate the performance based on those benchmarks

 - Compare filtering vs unfiltered images

 - Compare across distinct combinations and parameters of filtering

- Performance metrics for each **method** combination.

- Insights into which preprocessing techniques **help or hinder**
 > segmentation and phenotyping
